titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 168
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

p.note: The 4 parchment leaves in the beginning and 3 at the end are probably old flyleaves. They are of a much thicker and rougher quality than the leaves of the textblock. The first flyleaf at the end has been foliated .

supportDesc.foliation: Modern foliation in pencil. misterio absconditur

p.note: Top left corner, in black ink by a modern hand: A 168; upper parf of page in black ink by a modern hand: Aflemnad från Rikz Arkivet 8 Juni 1883. 609 blad underbart fint pergmanet med underbart fin stil. Bibeltexten upptar 1117 sidor (blad 4v-562) Hieronymi epistola: blad 1-4r. Register: blad 563-609. Nya testementet börjar 452v. Initialer med menniskor och djur = 90 (60 i gamla 30 i nya testam.) Ungefär lika många utan bilder. Flera hundra små endast röda eller blå. Bottom left corner, in pencil by a modern hand: measurements of the manuscript (smudged).
Centre page, in ink by a modern/early modern hand: Biblia Latine cum prologo S. Hieronymi.
Brown ink by a medieval hand: list of books in the Old testament from Genesis to Job.
Bottom left corner, in ink by a modern hand: S. W: 1954.

binding.p: Later medieval or early modern binding. Dark brown leather over wooden boards. 5 double raised bands and endbands. 2 straps with decorated hooked clasps, decorated studs on catchplates and strap attachments. , Stamped in gold: BIBLIA LATINA and A 168.

decoNote.p: Blind tooled decoration consisting of panels and complex knot work.

binding.condition: Spine renovated with new leather. One strap missing. and , close to the outer edge, one hole above the upper clasp and three holes between the claps (perhaps attachement points). The clasps and studs are possibly later additions.

provenance.p: Origin and early provenance unknown. In the late 16th century the manuscript belonged to Paul Petau. It was then acquired from his collection by Queen Christina in (not included in Vossius's catalogue of 1651). Later the manuscript ended up in the National Archives.
It is believed that the numbers 26.28 on and the refer to the Petau library.
According to a former librarian's note the manuscript may be related (same scribe or school) to MS A 167.

acquisition.p: Transferred from the National Archives to the National Library .

msItem.title: Prologue
msItem.rubric: Incipit epistula sancti ieronimi presbiteri ad paulinum de omnibus diuine historie libris
msItem.incipit: FRater ambrosius michi tua munuscula preferens detulit

msItem.title: Prologue to Kings
msItem.incipit: UIginti et duas litteras esse apud hebreos

msItem.title: Prologue to 2 Paralipomenon
msItem.incipit: Eusebius ieronimus dominioni et rogaciano suis

msItem.title: Prologue to Tobias
msItem.incipit: Chromacio et helyodoro episcopis Mirari non desino

msItem.title: Prologue to Job
msItem.incipit: COgor per singulos scripture diuine libros

msItem.title: Prologue to Ecclesiasticus
msItem.incipit: MVltorum nobis et magnorum per legem

msItem.title: Prologue to Jeremias
msItem.incipit: IEremias propheta cui hic prologus scribitur

msItem.title: Prologue to Baruch
msItem.incipit: LIber iste qui baruch nomine prenotatur

msItem.title: Prologue to Ezechiel
msItem.incipit: EZechiel propheta cum ioachim rege iuda

msItem.title: Prologue to Osee
msItem.incipit: TEmporibus ozie et ioathe achaz ezechie

msItem.title: Prologue to Amos
msItem.incipit: OZias rex cum dei cum religionem sollicite emularetur

msItem.title: Prologue to Micheas
msItem.incipit: Temporibus ioathe et achaz et ezechie regum Iuda

msItem.title: Prologue to Habacuc
msItem.incipit: QVatuor prophete in xii prophetarum uolumine sunt

msItem.title: Prologue to Machabees
msItem.incipit: DOmino excellentissimo et in cultu christiane religionis strenuissimo ludouico regi

msItem.title: Prologue to Machabees
msItem.incipit: REuerendissimo et omni caritatis officio dignissimo geroldo

msItem.title: Prologue to Matthew
msItem.incipit: MAtheus ex iudea sicut in ordine primus ponitur

msItem.title: Prologue to Luke
msItem.incipit: LUcas syrus natione antiochensis arte medicus
msItem.note:
The first prologue is Luke 1:1-1:4. The second: RB
.

msItem.title: Prologue to James
msItem.incipit: NOn est ita ordo apud grecos

supportDesc.foliation: Running titles in blue and red ink.
supportDesc.condition: Edges trimmed, resulting in partial loss of running titles.

handDesc.handNote: Late pregothic or early Gothic textualis.

p.note: Occasional additions with signe-de-renvoi by the scribe, e.g. and . Occasional marks (nota marks?) and short additions by a later hand in the margins.

decoDesc.p: Main text in brown in, rubrics in red, capitals touched in red.

decoNote.p: Each indivdual book is introduced by a historiated inital over 5 lines. The intials are outlined in black, painted in a mauve with white highlights. They are placed on a background of blue with black outlines. The counterspace contains the historiated image related to the Bible book it introduces. Parts of the letter or the background often extend into the margin and may contain animal, knotwork or other geometric shapes, e.g. and . Besides the begining of each new book the opening prologue also features a historiated intial. The Psalms contain 8 historiated intials. : opening intial I, extending along the entire page. : Historiated inital L over 23 lines introducing the New Testament. The prologues are introduced by a decorated initials over 5 lines, which feature animal and vegetative ornaments. The prologue to Proverbs on contains the largest and most advanced form of this type of intial. Individual paragraphs are introduced by plain initials over 2 lines in alternating blue and red with flourishing in the contrasting colour. The initials and their flourishing often extend along the margin to create a border, e.g. .
